archived_tasks.title: Fix `samples` generation (51 failures)
archived_tasks.description: All 51 sample test assertions fail because no sample files are emitted. The `SampleFiles` component (src/components/sample-files.tsx) returns undefined because `collectSampleInfos()` finds no operations with examples. TCGC's `operation.examples` array is empty even though the test scenarios define examples in JSON blocks. Root cause: The test scenarios define examples in `json for OperationId` blocks but TCGC isn't parsing these into the `operation.examples` array. Need to either: (a) ensure examples are properly loaded by TCGC, or (b) wire up example data from the test harness into the emitter.

archived_tasks.title: Fix `operations` category resolution for grouped operations (17 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 17 tests fail with 'No output file found for legacy category operations'. The test harness resolves the 'operations' category using regex `/api\/operations\.ts$/` which matches `src/api/operations.ts`. But the new emitter generates operations at grouped paths like `src/api/<group>/operations.ts` (e.g., `src/api/widgets/operations.ts`, `src/api/queryOperations/operations.ts`). The regex doesn't match these paths. Fix options: (a) update the harness regex to match grouped operation files, (b) concatenate all operation files for the 'operations' category, or (c) change the emitter to optionally generate flat operations files.

archived_tasks.title: Fix `models:withOptions` category resolution (14 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 14 tests fail with 'No output file found for legacy category models:withOptions'. The test harness resolves 'models:withOptions' using regex `/api\/options\.ts$/` expecting a file like `src/api/options.ts`. The new emitter doesn't generate a separate options file; operation options interfaces are generated inside the operation files (e.g., `src/api/<group>/operations.ts`). Fix options: (a) update the harness to look for options in operation files, (b) generate a separate options.ts file, or (c) update the harness to concatenate operation files and search for option interfaces within them.

archived_tasks.title: Implement response header deserialization functions (11 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 11 tests fail because header deserialization functions are not generated. Two types of functions are expected: (a) `_xxxDeserializeHeaders` for response headers on successful operations (e.g., `_getUserDeserializeHeaders`, `_getAccountInfoDeserializeHeaders`), and (b) `_xxxDeserializeExceptionHeaders` for error response headers (e.g., `_getWidgetDeserializeExceptionHeaders`). These functions extract typed header values from HTTP response headers (including conversion of strings to boolean, date, bytes). Currently, `deserialize-operation.tsx` only handles response body deserialization. This requires: (1) new DeserializeHeaders component, (2) new DeserializeExceptionHeaders component, (3) integration in operation-files.tsx, (4) support for `include-headers-in-response` config option from TCGC.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: Could not find function "_*Deserialize*Headers" in file "src/api/operations.ts"

archived_tasks.title: Fix enum union type alias generation (4 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 4 tests fail because named union type aliases (LR, UD, LeftAndRight, UpAndDown) are not emitted in models.ts. The test scenario enumUnion/enumUnion.md expects type aliases like `export type LR = "left" | "right"`. The model-files.tsx correctly filters for `SdkUnionType` from `sdkPackage.unions`, and `UnionDeclaration` renders named unions. The issue is likely that these unions are either: (a) not being included in the `unions` array by TCGC, (b) being classified differently (e.g., as enum types), or (c) being filtered out by the `u.kind === "union"` check when they have enum members.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: Could not find alias "*" in file "src/models/models.ts"

archived_tasks.title: Fix model-only package root index generation (3 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 3 tests fail because root index.ts is not generated for model-only packages (no operations, just models). In `index-file.tsx`, `RootIndexFile()` returns undefined when `clients.length === 0`. For model-only packages with `@usage` annotation, models.ts IS generated but root index.ts is not. The fix: modify `RootIndexFile()` to generate index.ts even without clients if models/enums/unions exist. Also handle the case where no models should be generated (without @usage) — the test expects an empty output with only helper files.
archived_tasks.error_pattern: No output file found for legacy category "root index" / "models"
archived_tasks.notes: Fixed by modifying RootIndexFile() and IndexFiles() to generate root index and models index for model-only packages (no clients). Also updated scenario harness to gracefully handle missing files with sentinel comment.

archived_tasks.title: Fix multipart request type underscore prefix (2 failures)
archived_tasks.description: 2 tests fail because the multipart request type `_UploadFileRequest` and its serializer `_uploadFileRequestSerializer` are generated without the underscore prefix. The multipart model interface is generated in model-files.tsx and serialized via multipart-serializer.tsx. Need to detect when a model is an internal/anonymous request type and prefix its name with underscore.

archived_tasks.title: API version types emitted as union type alias instead of KnownVersions enum

archived_tasks.title: Enum member name normalization differs from legacy PascalCase rules

archived_tasks.title: Additional properties modeled as index signature instead of explicit field

archived_tasks.title: Polymorphic serializers missing base model fallback
archived_tasks.root_cause: json-polymorphic-serializer.tsx generates switch statements where the default case returns 'item' as-is. Legacy generates a base model serializer/deserializer and calls it in the default case. Also, child deserializers don't include inherited parent properties.

archived_tasks.title: plainDate serialization uses full ISO string instead of date-only

archived_tasks.title: Unix timestamp encoding uses milliseconds instead of seconds

archived_tasks.title: Nested enum types flattened to string literal union instead of composed types
archived_tasks.root_cause: sub-enum-declaration.tsx flattens all enum values into a single string literal union. Legacy generates separate type aliases (e.g., ResourceProvisioningState, Baz) composed into unions with '| string' for extensibility, plus dedicated serializer functions.

archived_tasks.title: Classical client missing constructor overloads for subscription handling
archived_tasks.root_cause: classical-client.tsx generates a single constructor. Legacy generates overloaded constructors when a service has mixed tenant-level and subscription-level operations, with polymorphic subscriptionIdOrOptions parameter handling.

archived_tasks.title: Reserved word methods use trailing underscore instead of dollar prefix
archived_tasks.root_cause: classical-client.tsx uses method.name directly without reserved word escaping. The TCGC layer may be providing names with _ suffix. Legacy uses $ prefix ($continue, $return) internally while exposing the bare name publicly.
archived_tasks.description: Public method names for reserved words differ: continue_ (NEW) vs continue (LEGACY). Internal function names also differ: _continueSend (NEW) vs _$continueSend (LEGACY).

archived_tasks.title: Nullable types preserve | null in optional properties
archived_tasks.root_cause: type-expression.tsx appends '| null' for nullable types. When combined with optional (?:), this produces 'prop?: T | null'. Legacy strips the null from optional nullable types, producing 'prop?: T'.
archived_tasks.description: Optional nullable properties keep the | null union. Legacy convention treats optional as implicitly nullable, so '| null' is redundant and stripped.

archived_tasks.title: Multipart file types use File interface hierarchy instead of FileContents union
archived_tasks.root_cause: multipart-serializer.tsx generates structured File interfaces (File, FileRequiredName extends File, PngFile extends File) with dedicated serializers. Legacy generates FileContents union types (FileContents | { contents: FileContents; ... }).

archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: TypeSpec now provides a native File model type (Http.File) with contents/filename/contentType fields. The File interface hierarchy (File, FileRequiredName extends File, PngFile extends File) is the correct modern approach, replacing the legacy FileContents union workaround. The contentType parameter comes from the TypeSpec definition (@header contentType). All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: API version passed as explicit function parameter instead of context property
archived_tasks.root_cause: send-operation.tsx excludes apiVersion from function parameters (line 178: isApiVersionParam filter) and reads it from context.apiVersion. However, in some scenarios the NEW output shows apiVersion as a function parameter. Inconsistency between send-operation logic and test expectations.

archived_tasks.resolution: Fixed by: (1) Adding withVersionedApiVersion support to test harness to inject @versioned(Versions) decorator and Versions enum, causing TCGC to correctly mark apiVersion as a client-level parameter; (2) Adding default value fallback (context.apiVersion ?? "defaultValue") in send-operation.tsx for client-level apiVersion with clientDefaultValue.

archived_tasks.title: Client class and context naming ignores service title configuration
archived_tasks.root_cause: classical-client.tsx uses client.name from TCGC directly. When typespec-title-map or @clientName decorators rename the client, the generated sample code may not reflect the renamed class. Also affects context function naming (createService vs createTestService).

archived_tasks.title: Azure flavor not applied in scenario test expectations

archived_tasks.description: Test infrastructure needs to set flavor='azure' for scenarios that expect Azure SDK imports. The emitter supports both flavors but tests may default to 'core'.

archived_tasks.title: Import aliases with _1 suffix in generated operations code
archived_tasks.root_cause: Alloy's name conflict resolution generates _1 suffixed aliases when multiple declarations share the same name across files. Legacy uses direct imports without aliases. This may be caused by redundant refkey registrations or naming conflicts in the component tree.
archived_tasks.description: All imported types and functions get _1 suffixes (Client_1, StreamableMethod_1, fooDeserializer_1) in operations files, which doesn't match legacy output.

archived_tasks.title: Fix flatten property wire format in serializer and deserializer
archived_tasks.root_cause: json-serializer.tsx expands flatten properties inline via getSerializableProperties(), placing them at the top level of the serialized object. The correct wire format wraps them under the original property name (e.g., 'properties: { bar, baz }' not 'bar, baz'). Similarly, json-deserializer.tsx reads from top-level item['bar'] instead of nested item['properties']['bar'].
archived_tasks.description: Modify JsonSerializer to iterate model.properties directly, generating nested ObjectExpression under the original wire name for flatten properties. For optional flatten, use areAllPropsUndefined helper. Modify JsonDeserializer to read flatten properties from the nested wire location (item[wireName][nestedSerializedName]). This is the core wire format correctness fix.

archived_tasks.title: Fix name collision resolution for flattened properties
archived_tasks.root_cause: When a parent model has properties with the same name as flattened properties, the current emitter produces duplicate property names in interfaces and serializers (invalid TypeScript). Legacy resolves collisions by prefixing with the flatten property path (e.g., 'bar' from 'properties' becomes 'barPropertiesBar').
archived_tasks.description: Implement name collision detection in getExpandedProperties() and getSerializerEntries(). When a flattened property name collides with an existing property, rename it with path prefix (propName + flattenPropName + PropName pattern). Update serializer to map prefixed client names to original wire names. Update deserializer similarly.

archived_tasks.title: Generate helper serializer/deserializer functions for flatten groups
archived_tasks.root_cause: Legacy emitter generates separate exported helper functions (_modelPropertiesSerializer, _modelPropertiesDeserializer) for each flatten property group. The new emitter (after RC30a fix) will inline the nested object. Generating separate helpers achieves output parity.
archived_tasks.description: Instead of inlining nested object expressions for flatten groups, generate separate FunctionDeclaration components (_modelPropSerializer, _modelPropDeserializer) and reference them from the main serializer/deserializer via refkeys. The deserializer uses spread syntax to merge helper results.

archived_tasks.title: Missing ESLint disable directives in model output files
archived_tasks.root_cause: model-interface.tsx generates JSDoc via the doc prop but does not emit eslint-disable comments (naming-convention, explicit-module-boundary-types) at the top of model files. Legacy includes these as file-level comments.
archived_tasks.description: Model files lack eslint-disable directives that legacy includes for generated code compatibility.

archived_tasks.title: Response types use intersection instead of inline expanded object
archived_tasks.root_cause: deserialize-headers.tsx composes response types as 'ModelType & { header: type }' intersections. Legacy expands all properties into a single inline object type. Semantically equivalent but different code style.
archived_tasks.description: Response types with headers use type intersection (User & { requestId: string }) instead of fully expanded inline objects.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: Intersection types (User & { requestId: string }) are semantically equivalent to inline expanded objects in TypeScript. The intersection approach is cleaner, more maintainable, and correctly composes model types with response headers. All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Constant model properties expanded as function parameters
archived_tasks.root_cause: When all model properties are constants, send-operation.tsx explodes them into function parameters with literal types instead of hardcoding values in the request body.
archived_tasks.description: Constant-only models generate function parameters with literal types. Legacy hardcodes constant values directly in the request body.
archived_tasks.resolution: Accepted divergence: When a model is spread (...Model) into operation parameters, TCGC models each property as an individual operation parameter, including constants. The emitter faithfully renders TCGC data model. Literal types ensure type safety. All scenario tests pass.

archived_tasks.title: Constructor endpoint parameter named 'endpoint' instead of 'endpointParam'
archived_tasks.root_cause: client-context.tsx uses the TCGC template argument name directly for the parameter. Legacy appends 'Param' suffix to avoid potential conflicts with the options interface property.
archived_tasks.description: Constructor parameter is named 'endpoint' instead of 'endpointParam'. Minor naming convention difference.

archived_tasks.title: JSDoc Comments: Expanded @param Tags and Description Text Changes
archived_tasks.description: The new emitter generates different JSDoc content — expanded @param annotations, 'Optional parameters for the X operation' instead of 'Optional parameters.', removal of eslint-disable comments, multi-line formatting. Review if legacy JSDoc format must be matched exactly.
archived_tasks.legacy_behavior: Minimal JSDoc: '/** Optional parameters. */' with eslint-disable comments, single-line format.
archived_tasks.new_behavior: Expanded JSDoc with @param tags, operation-specific descriptions, multi-line format.
archived_tasks.decision: Accept improvement. Expanded JSDoc with @param tags and operation-specific descriptions is better documentation. No need to match legacy minimal JSDoc.

archived_tasks.title: Make ignoreNullableOnOptional default flavor-aware
archived_tasks.description:
In src/emitter.tsx line 184, ignoreNullableOnOptional currently defaults to true for all flavors (context.options?.['ignore-nullable-on-optional'] !== false). The legacy emitter defaults this to true only for Azure flavor and false for core. Update: if user explicitly sets the option, use their value; otherwise default to true for Azure, false for core. The flavor is already resolved at line 178 before emitterOptions is constructed.

Legacy ref: submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/src/transform/transfromRLCOptions.ts lines 275-285.

When flavor='core' and ignore-nullable-on-optional is NOT set, ignoreNullableOnOptional defaults to false

archived_tasks.title: Generate pass-through (identity) serializer/deserializer functions
archived_tasks.description:
The new emitter omits serializer functions for types that are identity operations (e.g., models where all properties are simple scalars). The legacy emitter generates these as `function fooSerializer(item) { return item; }`. Consumers may import these functions, so omitting them is a breaking change.

The issue is in the serializer/deserializer generation pipeline. When `needsTransformation()` returns false for a model, no serializer function is generated. Instead, a pass-through serializer should still be emitted.

Key files:
- src/components/serialization/json-serializer.tsx — `needsTransformation()` function (lines 290-316) determines if a type needs transformation. Models always return true, but the issue may be in how model-files.tsx filters which models get serializers.
- src/components/model-files.tsx — lines 126-149, filters models by UsageFlags.Input for serializers and UsageFlags.Output for deserializers.
- The filtering may be too aggressive — some models with only simple properties may need serializer functions even if the body is just `return item;`.

Legacy ref: submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/src/modular/ — serializer generation always emits a function for each model.

Scenario audit: SA-C13.

archived_tasks.title: Match legacy binary response handling: direct return or getBinaryResponse
archived_tasks.description:
The new emitter uses `stringToUint8Array(result.body, 'base64')` for binary responses. The legacy emitter either returns body directly or uses a `getBinaryResponse()` helper wrapper that calls the streaming method to prevent the Core from doing a UTF-8 decode on raw bytes.

The issue is in how binary (bytes with encode='bytes') responses are deserialized. The legacy uses a special path:
1. Instead of awaiting the send function normally, it captures the StreamableMethod
2. Passes it to `getBinaryResponse(streamableMethod)` which handles binary streaming
3. This prevents the HTTP pipeline from corrupting binary data with UTF-8 decoding

Key files:
- src/components/deserialize-operation.tsx — response body handling
- src/components/serialization/json-deserializer.tsx lines 248-261 — bytes case uses stringToUint8Array
- Legacy ref: submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/src/modular/helpers/operationHelpers.ts lines 895-912 — getBinaryResponse helper pattern
- Legacy ref: submodules/autorest.typescript/packages/typespec-ts/src/modular/static-helpers-metadata.ts lines 57-61 — getBinaryResponse helper definition

Scenario audit: SA-C19.

No stringToUint8Array conversion on binary response bodies

archived_tasks.title: Fix [object Object] in classic operation group interface method signatures
archived_tasks.description:
In src/components/classical-operation-groups.tsx, the buildMethodParamList() function builds an array of Alloy Children objects (from code template literals) and then calls .join(", ") on them. JavaScript's .join() calls .toString() on each element, and Alloy Children objects render as [object Object]. This produces broken interface signatures like: list: (options?: ,[object Object]) => Promise<(Widget)[]>.

The fix: replace `return code`${parts.join(", ")}`; with proper Alloy-idiomatic Children composition. For example, use a mapped approach: `return code`${parts.map((p, i) => (i > 0 ? code`, ${p}` : p))}`; or use Alloy's <For> component with comma separator. The key constraint is that Children arrays must not be .join()'d — they must be composed via code template interpolation or JSX.

Verification: After fixing, run `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke` and confirm that src/classic/widgets/index.ts in temp/smoke/http-client-js/ has proper TypeScript function signatures in the WidgetsOperations interface (e.g., `list: (options?: ListOptionalParams) => Promise<Widget[]>`).

Also run `pnpm test` to ensure existing unit tests still pass.

archived_tasks.title: Fix hardcoded endpoint variable name ignoring name policy transformation
archived_tasks.description:
The TCGC parameter name 'endpoint' is transformed to 'endpointParam' by the name policy (src/utils/name-policy.ts) because 'endpoint' is a reserved parameter name that gets a 'Param' suffix. However, two components hardcode the raw name 'endpoint' in generated code instead of using the transformed name:

(1) src/components/client-context.tsx, buildEndpointFromType() function, lines 740 and 743:
```
parts.push(code`const endpointUrl = options.endpoint ?? endpoint;`);
```
Both the case where arg.clientDefaultValue is defined and the else branch hardcode 'endpoint'. The fix: use the actual parameter name. Since this is inside buildEndpointFromType which receives an SdkEndpointParameter, the hasOnlyEndpoint branch (lines 736-744) should reference the transformed parameter name. Note that `arg.name` gives the raw TCGC name 'endpoint', not the name-policy-transformed 'endpointParam'. You need to either use a namekey/refkey approach or manually apply the naming convention.

(2) src/components/classical-client.tsx, buildNonOptionsArguments() function, line 343:
```
args.push(arg.name);
```
This pushes the raw TCGC endpoint template argument name 'endpoint' into the factory call arguments. But the constructor parameter is declared through Alloy's ParameterDescriptor which applies name policy, producing 'endpointParam'. The factory call then uses 'endpoint' which is undefined. The fix: apply the same name transformation or use the getEscapedParameterName() helper from name-policy.ts.

Generated output showing the bug:
- src/demoServiceClient.ts constructor: `this._client = createDemoService(endpoint, { ... })` — 'endpoint' is undefined, should be 'endpointParam'
- src/demoServiceClientContext.ts: `const endpointUrl = options.endpoint ?? endpoint;` — 'endpoint' is undefined, should be 'endpointParam'

Verification: Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke`. In temp/smoke/http-client-js/, confirm that demoServiceClient.ts and demoServiceClientContext.ts reference 'endpointParam' (or whatever the name policy produces) consistently. Also run `pnpm test`.

archived_tasks.title: Fix expandUrlTemplate missing from @typespec/ts-http-runtime
archived_tasks.description:
In src/utils/external-packages.ts line 56, the httpRuntimeLib external package descriptor declares 'expandUrlTemplate' as a named export of @typespec/ts-http-runtime. However, this function does NOT exist in @typespec/ts-http-runtime@0.2.1. The generated operations file imports it, causing TS2305: Module has no exported member 'expandUrlTemplate'.

The emitter uses expandUrlTemplate in src/components/send-operation.tsx to build URL template expansion (e.g., replacing {id} in /widgets/{id} with the actual parameter value). The generated code is:
```
const path = expandUrlTemplate("/widgets/{id}", { "id": id }, { allowReserved: options?.requestOptions?.skipUrlEncoding });
```

Possible fixes:
1. If expandUrlTemplate will be added to a newer version of @typespec/ts-http-runtime, update the package version dependency in the smoke test scaffold (check test/smoke/ for the package.json template or tspconfig.yaml).
2. If the function exists under a different name or in a different package, update the external-packages.ts descriptor.
3. If the function needs to be emitted as a static helper (like serializationHelpers.ts), add it to the static helpers.
4. Check submodules/typespec/ to see if a newer version of the runtime exports this function.

Verification: Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke`. Confirm that src/api/widgets/operations.ts in temp/smoke/http-client-js/ compiles without the expandUrlTemplate import error. Also run `pnpm test`.

archived_tasks.title: Fix generic type parameter T lowercased to t by name policy in XML helpers
archived_tasks.description:
In src/components/static-helpers/xml-helpers.tsx, the DeserializeFromXmlFunction and DeserializeXmlObjectFunction components declare typeParameters={["T"]} (uppercase T) on their FunctionDeclaration. However, the generated output has lowercase 't' in the function signature (e.g., `deserializeFromXml<t>`) while the code body still uses uppercase 'T' (e.g., `deserializeXmlObject<T>(...)` and `return result as T`). This produces TS2552: Cannot find name 'T'. Did you mean 't'?

The root cause is likely the emitter's name policy (src/utils/name-policy.ts, createEmitterNamePolicy()). The name policy applies casing transformations to declarations, and type parameters may be getting lowercased. The fix is to either:
1. Mark the type parameter with ignoreNamePolicy so it preserves the uppercase T: use namekey("T", { ignoreNamePolicy: true }) or equivalent.
2. Make the code body references match the transformed name (use lowercase 't' everywhere).
3. Adjust the name policy to not transform type parameters.

Option 1 is preferred since uppercase single-letter type parameters (T, K, V) are the standard TypeScript convention.

Verification: Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke`. Confirm that src/static-helpers/xmlHelpers.ts in temp/smoke/http-client-js/ has consistent casing for the generic type parameter (either all T or all t, but T is preferred). Also run `pnpm test`.

archived_tasks.title: Fix classic operation group file import paths off by one directory level
archived_tasks.description:
The generated file src/classic/widgets/index.ts has Alloy-resolved imports (via refkeys) that are one directory level too shallow. All imports use '../' (resolving to src/classic/) instead of '../../' (resolving to src/):

```typescript
import { $delete, ... } from "../api/widgets/operations.js";        // resolves to src/classic/api/... WRONG
import type { DemoServiceContext } from "../demoServiceClientContext.js"; // resolves to src/classic/demoService... WRONG
import type { Widget } from "../models/models.js";                   // resolves to src/classic/models/... WRONG
```

These are NOT manually constructed — they're auto-generated by Alloy's refkey resolution system. The bug is likely in the component tree structure. In the emitter tree (src/emitter.tsx), two separate <SourceDirectory path="classic"> nodes exist:
1. ClassicalOperationGroupFiles (src/components/classical-operation-groups.tsx line 89) wraps its SourceFiles in <SourceDirectory path="classic">
2. IndexFiles (src/components/index-file.tsx line 302) wraps <ClassicIndexFile /> in a separate <SourceDirectory path="classic">

Having two separate SourceDirectory nodes with the same path may confuse Alloy's path resolution. The files inside one SourceDirectory may not be aware of the correct nesting depth.

Possible fixes:
1. Merge both classic directories into a single <SourceDirectory path="classic"> that contains both the operation group files and the index file.
2. Move the <SourceDirectory path="classic"> wrapper out of ClassicalOperationGroupFiles and into the parent emitter.tsx, then have both ClassicalOperationGroupFiles (without its own wrapper) and ClassicIndexFile as siblings inside a single classic SourceDirectory.
3. Investigate if this is an Alloy framework bug with duplicate SourceDirectory paths.

Note: This error is currently MASKED by SMOKE-1's syntax errors (which prevent tsc from analyzing imports in the same file). Fix SMOKE-1 first, then verify this issue still exists.

Verification: Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke`. In temp/smoke/http-client-js/src/classic/widgets/index.ts, check that imports use '../../' prefix to correctly resolve to files under src/. Also run `pnpm test`.

archived_tasks.title: Fix empty classic/index.ts barrel file from unconfigured BarrelFile component
archived_tasks.description:
In src/components/index-file.tsx, ClassicIndexFile() (lines 237-239) returns a bare <BarrelFile /> with no configuration:

```typescript
export function ClassicIndexFile() {
  return <BarrelFile />;
}
```

This generates an empty src/classic/index.ts file. The BarrelFile component auto-discovers and re-exports symbols from sibling SourceFiles within its SourceDirectory. However, since the ClassicIndexFile is rendered inside its own <SourceDirectory path="classic"> (index-file.tsx line 302) which is SEPARATE from the <SourceDirectory path="classic"> that wraps the actual operation group files (classical-operation-groups.tsx line 89), the BarrelFile has no sibling symbols to discover.

This is closely related to SMOKE-6 (duplicate SourceDirectory nodes). The fix for SMOKE-6 (merging into a single SourceDirectory) should also fix this issue — once the ClassicIndexFile and the operation group files share the same SourceDirectory, the BarrelFile should auto-discover the exported symbols (WidgetsOperations, _getWidgetsOperations, etc.).

Alternatively, if the BarrelFile approach doesn't work after merging, manually build the classic barrel exports similar to how ApiIndexFile and RootIndexFile work — using buildExportStatement with collected operation group interface names.

Expected output for src/classic/index.ts:
```typescript
export { WidgetsOperations, _getWidgetsOperations } from "./widgets/index.js";
```

Verification: Run `pnpm build && pnpm test:smoke`. Confirm src/classic/index.ts in temp/smoke/http-client-js/ is non-empty and re-exports operation group interfaces. Also run `pnpm test`.
